taxonomy: Alicosta alpina is a species within the family Sesiidae, commonly known as the clearwing moths. This group of moths is further classified within the order Lepidoptera, which consists of butterflies and moths. As a member of the Sesiidae family, Alicosta alpina shares characteristics with other clearwing moths, such as their long, slender body shape and distinctive clear wings. The species is also classified within the genus Alicosta, which is a part of the larger family Sesiidae. This complex classification highlights the intricate relationships between different species within the Lepidoptera order.
